channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: Exosomes in cancer drug resistance: dual roles in therapy failure and emerging precision therapeutics
item.description: This review examines the dual roles of exosomes in cancer drug resistance, exploring how these extracellular vesicles contribute to therapy failure while simultaneously presenting opportunities for precision therapeutic development. The article discusses mechanistic pathways through which exosomes m

item.title: STAT+: Telemedicine company touted by Novo Nordisk stressed profits over patient safety, ex-workers say
item.description: LifeMD, a popular telehealth company, has been accused by former employees of pressing clinicians to mete out prescriptions more rapidly without sufficient screening and follow-up.

item.title: How nature could inspire ai’s next breakthrough
item.description: RIYADH: For years, the race in artificial intelligence has been driven by scale, with companies building ever-・・・

item.title: Kalshi expands to drug trial bets as prediction markets surge
item.description: Kalshi launches betting on drug trial results and FDA decisions, aiming to surface hidden data. Critics warn of manipulation and insider trading risks.

item.title: Mercy health encourages early detection duringsarcoma & bone cancer awareness month
item.description: Persistent pain, swelling or a lump that doesn't go away is often caused by a sports injury, arthritis or another common condition. But in rare cases, these symptoms may signal sarcoma or bone cancer. During Sarcoma and Bone Cancer Awareness Month this Ju

item.title: Can public health become precise? - this week in public health
item.description: Can public health become as targeted as precision medicine? Explore the promise, challenges, and future of precision public health.

item.title: Neil rimer thinks the AI money is coming back out | techcrunch
item.description: Neil Rimer, the venture capitalist who co-founded Index Ventures, predicts the historic wealth AI is generating in Silicon Valley will have to be redistributed, voluntarily or involuntarily.

item.title: Defining safety parameters for first-trimester malaria treatment trials: stakeholder views from Real-Time Delphi survey
item.description: This study used a Real-Time Delphi survey to gather stakeholder perspectives on defining appropriate safety parameters for clinical trials evaluating malaria treatments during the first trimester of pregnancy. The research synthesizes expert consensus across multiple disciplines to establish evidenc

item.title: Beyond one-size-fits-all: Latent patient classes at methadone treatment entry and their clinical implications - Evidence from a large real-world cohort
item.description: This real-world cohort study identifies distinct patient subgroups at the point of methadone treatment initiation using latent class analysis, revealing heterogeneous clinical profiles that have implications for personalized treatment approaches. The findings challenge standardized one-size-fits-all

item.title: Artificial Intelligence and Protein Design: A retrospective study on 20-year emerging trends and core research areas from bibliometric perspectives
item.description: This bibliometric analysis examines 20 years of research trends at the intersection of artificial intelligence and protein design, identifying core research areas and emerging developments in the field. The study systematically maps the evolution of scientific publications and research focus areas t

item.title: Combination cardiometabolic therapy in type 2 diabetes: optimizing SGLT2 inhibitor and GLP‑1 receptor agonist use
item.description: This review examines the combined use of SGLT2 inhibitors and GLP-1 receptor agonists in managing type 2 diabetes, focusing on optimizing cardiometabolic outcomes through dual-agent therapy strategies.

item.title: Nps onboarding redesign, ai-powered pension advisory platform, nps swasthya—pfrda chair encourages ‘responsible’ AI use
item.description: PFRDA Chairman S Ramann outlined an AI roadmap to redesign the National Pension Scheme's onboarding process. He also encouraged ‘responsible’ AI use for a pension advisory platform powered by the technology.

item.title: The 2p pill that could slash your risk of arthritis by a quarter
item.description: Metformin has been used since the 1950s to treat diabetes , but a new study of 350,000 Britons, shows it reduces the risk of knee and hip osteoarthritis - wear and tear of the joints - by 24 per cent.

item.title: How close are we to quicker endometriosis tests? a women's health expert explains
item.description: The movement is good news, but more complicated than headlines suggest.

item.title: Kimi: threat or menace? | techcrunch
item.description: Chinese company Moonshot AI released a new version of its Kimi model this week, prompting concern about "full AI communism."

item.title: Your period tracker is (probably) spying on you
item.description: Plus: Russian cyberspies turn to infrastructure hacking, DHS repeatedly fails to realize it’d been hacked, a breach exposes an AI music generator’s scraping ways, and more.

item.title: Prompt injection attacks are thwarting AI hacking agents
item.description: “Context bombing” tricks malicious AI agents into shutting down before they can do harm.

item.title: Enforcing all those new state-level AI laws is unlikely and thus opens the door to foul results
item.description: U.S. states are rapidly passing new AI laws. But if the enforcement is weak or nonexistent, these laws will be hollow. An AI Insider analysis and scoop.

item.title: I attended the kiro and amazon quick fiesta event in malaysia
item.description: AWS's first agentic AI event in Malaysia showcased Kiro and Amazon Quick — two products designed to turn AI agents from demos into production workflows. Here's what happened, what I learned, and why the workflow framing matters more than the speed-up promises.

item.title: A diarrhea-causing parasite is spreading. should you stop eating salad?
item.description: Salad greens could be a source of the growing cyclospora outbreak, but officials haven't identified a specific product, grower or supplier.

item.title: Join us: building secure healthcare systems
item.description: Register for our July 30 panel on reducing risk through better architecture, AI governance, and developer workflows, featuring David Pace of Merck and Joël Quenneville and Clarissa Borges of thoughtbot.

item.title: South korea making its own security-centric AI model
item.description: Adapting existing local LLM project for security and sovereignty purposes and hopes to one day match Mythos

item.title: Non-Pharmacological Interventions for Postoperative Pain in Breast Cancer Patients: A Network Meta-analysis
item.description: This network meta-analysis evaluates the effectiveness of non-pharmacological interventions for managing postoperative pain in breast cancer patients. The study synthesizes evidence from multiple treatment approaches to identify which interventions provide the most effective pain relief in this pati

item.title: Safety and efficacy of metformin for the treatment of pain: protocol for a systematic review
item.description: This protocol describes a planned systematic review to evaluate the safety and efficacy of metformin in treating pain. The review will synthesize evidence on metformin's use as a pain management intervention, with assessment of both therapeutic benefits and safety outcomes. This work addresses an em

item.title: A multicentre randomised, double-blind, double-dummy phase II clinical trial of benznidazole versus nifurtimox in adults with chronic Chagas disease in Brazil, alongside a prospective cohort: the BENBRASIL trial protocol
item.description: This protocol describes a multicentre, randomised, double-blind, double-dummy phase II clinical trial comparing benznidazole and nifurtimox in adults with chronic Chagas disease in Brazil, integrated with a prospective cohort study. The BENBRASIL trial aims to evaluate the relative efficacy and safe

item.title: FDA Approves First Oral PCSK9 Inhibitor to Lower LDL Cholesterol in Adults with High Cholesterol
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration approved Lipfendra (enlicitide), the first oral inhibitor of proprotein convertase subtilisin/kexin type 9 (PCSK9), as an adjunct to diet and exercise to reduce low-density lipoprotein cholesterol (LDL-C), or “bad” cholesterol, in adults with hypercholesterolemi

item.title: STAT+: The end for GSK’s chronic cough treatment
item.description: Sonothera's ultrasound gene therapy delivery, a new Alzheimer's drug controversy, and more biotech news from The Readout

item.title: The isomorphic labs drug design engine unlocks a new frontier beyond alphafold
item.description: Today, we are excited to share an update on our progress towards a new frontier of drug design. We have unlocked a new paradigm of predictive accuracy in understanding our biomolecular world, allowing us to rationally design new medicines on a computer with u…

item.title: Alphafold is evidence of a computational universe
item.description: Standard view treats physics as fundamental — matter, energy, fields — with information as merely a description we lay on top. Google Deepmind Demis Hassabis inverts this. Prior physicists John Wheeler’s “it from bit”, Konrad Zuse’s and Ed Fredkin’s digital p…

item.title: The AI backlash intensifies, china gains on the US leaders, and ibm tanks
item.description: You know a technology is getting serious acceptance when even people in the business shift from telling the government to butt out to calling for more regulation. This is where we’re at with artificial intelligence today. A raft of AI leaders and economists t…

item.title: Tech bills of the week: screen time standards for kids; teaching elementary school students about AI; modernizing agriculture and more
item.description: Bills this week aim to regulate how children interact with and learn about AI, give America’s farmers more AI equipment and add AI regulation to Medicare claim denials.

item.title: Rabobank and expert.ai renew seven-year AI partnership to transform banking automation and customer experience
item.description: Rabobank and Expert.ai have expanded their long-running artificial intelligence partnership, marking a new phase in the banking sector’s shift toward intelligent automation, better customer experiences, and responsible AI adoption. The renewed agreement build…

item.title: Neurology 2027 conference: paris event to focus on AI, brain health and mental disorders - long island guide
item.description: Attendees listen to presentations during a previous Scientex neurology conference. Neurology 2027 will bring neurologists, psychiatrists, neuroscientists, researchers, and healthcare professionals to Paris on April 19-20, 2027, to discuss advances in

item.title: Faster aging in younger people tied to rise in early-onset cancer
item.description: A new study suggests that younger generations are aging biologically faster than their older counterparts.

item.title: Agenus announces three bot+bal presentations spanning neoadjuvant and late-line colorectal cancer and advanced sarcomas at esmo 2026
item.description: LEXINGTON, Mass.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Jul 17, 2026-- AgenusInc. (Nasdaq: AGEN), a leader in immuno-oncology innovation, today announced that three abstracts from investigator-sponsored trials evaluating botensilimab and balstilimab (BOT+BAL) have been accepted f…

item.title: Obesity weighing down UK workforce – study
item.description: Research suggests obesity may keep more than 600,000 Britons out of work, with experts warning of wider economic costs and calling for targeted action

item.title: Do common dopaminergic variants modulate processing speed in cognitive aging? a longitudinal candidate gene study
item.description: Amid a global shift toward older populations, understanding the mechanisms of cognitive aging is a public health priority. Processing speed shows age-related decline and predicts dementia risk. Neuroimaging links dopaminergic system integrity to cognitive per…

item.title: The epidemiology of the viral hepatitis in brazil: a scoping review
item.description: Background Brazil has advanced toward eliminating viral hepatitis by 2030 through broad prevention, diagnosis, and treatment policies. Yet substantial gaps persist across the care continuum, especially in vulnerable populations and underserved regions. This s…

item.title: Long-term neurodevelopmental outcomes after vacuum-assisted delivery: a population-based cohort study
item.description: Author summary Why was this study done? Despite widespread use of vacuum-assisted delivery (VAD), concerns remain regarding possible long-term neurodevelopmental consequences for children, particularly after procedures performed at higher fetal head statio…

item.title: Herpesvirus reactivation is associated with mortality in critically ill icu patients with covid-19: insights from a retrospective single-center analysis of 455 cases
item.description: Background Critically ill patients with COVID-19 frequently develop viral reactivation, yet the clinical significance of herpes simplex virus type 1 (HSV-1) and cytomegalovirus (CMV) remains a matter of debate. Methods We conducted a retrospective single-ce…

item.title: A 2025 mit-led study mapped 32,000 skills across 923 occupations and found current AI tools can already perform work representing 11.7 per cent of america’s entire wage bill — about $1.2 trillion a year
item.description: A 2025 MIT-led study estimates that current AI tools overlap with skills representing 11.7 per cent of the United States wage bill, or roughly $1.2 trillion a year. That is a large number, but it needs careful wording. The researchers are not saying AI has al…

item.title: Stopping ozempic or wegovy leads to nearly 2 pounds of weight regain per month, largest review finds
item.description: Oxford study of 9,341 adults finds stopping Ozempic or Wegovy causes ~1.8 lbs of weight regain per month. What patients need to know about GLP-1 discontinuation.

item.title: How indigenous technologists are building data sovereignty into AI | betakit
item.description: Mila’s Indigenous AI Gathering explored how to create AI solutions without data extraction.

item.title: Chair's statement of the 2026 world artificial intelligence conference & high-level meeting on global AI governance
item.description: The 2026 World Artificial Intelligence Conference & High-Level Meeting on Global AI Governance was held in Shanghai, China from July 17 to 20. President Xi Jinping of the People's Republic of China attended the opening ceremony of the Conference and delivered…

item.title: 2026 government chemist conference report and recordings
item.description: Government Chemist Conference - Safe food for tomorrow’s world: evidence-based measurement solutions for responsible regulation (recordings available below)

item.title: The download: perimenopause misinformation and china’s latest AI leap
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. There’s a lot of hype around perimenopause. Don’t buy it. Perimenopause used to be considered taboo, but not anymore. Tha…

item.title: Why the first gpu financiers are turning to inference chips in a $400 million deal | techcrunch
item.description: Why the first GPU financiers are turning to inference chips in a $400 million deal | TechCrunchtechcrunch.com

item.title: Vertu wants executives to pay $6,880 for an AI agent — here's how it actually performs | techcrunch
item.description: From AI workflows to battery life and security, here's what it's really like to live with Vertu's luxury foldable every day.

item.title: A $10k bounty aims to make sony’s playstation 5 a computer again
item.description: Nice PlayStation you got. Bet it could probably run Linux too.

item.title: The risk of weather data sabotage is rising
item.description: Every morning, airline dispatchers, grid operators, and farmers around the world make decisions based on the same thing: a weather forecast. While these forecasts are something that most people glance at for two seconds, weather predictions influence major st…
